signals.headline: US Enforces Blockade on Iranian Ports After Failed Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Enforces Blockade on Iranian Ports After Failed Ceasefire Talks | NO | HIGH confidence**  
No ceasefire has been confirmed, announced, or resolved; instead, the US military began enforcing a blockade on all maritime traffic to/from Iranian ports on April 13, 2026, following the collapse of high-stakes ceasefire negotiations in Pakistan.[2]

signals.headline: Key inflation context:** Consumer prices recently climbed to approximately 2
signals.raw: Key inflation context:** Consumer prices recently climbed to approximately 2.2%, slightly above the central bank's 2% target, driven largely by surging oil costs.[5] The Policy Board expects inflation to rise to the mid to upper two-percent range this year.[5] Despite moderating inflation in January (which fell to the central bank's 2% target[1]), the subsequent March reading rose to 2.2%,[2] sugg

signals.headline: South Korea Keeps Benchmark Rate at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Keeps Benchmark Rate at 2.5% Amid Inflation Pressures | NO | HIGH confidence**

The Bank of Korea held its policy rate steady at 2.5% for the 7th consecutive month on Friday, April 10, 2026, in a unanimous decision by the Monetary Policy Board.[5] This decision maintains the status quo rather than confirming a significant policy shift.
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Market implications:** The decision aligns with market expectations and does not represent a major policy shift that wou
signals.raw: Market implications:** The decision aligns with market expectations and does not represent a major policy shift that would significantly move prediction markets. Analysts note that the BoK's easing cycle has ended and the rate is expected to remain at 2.5% for an extended period.[4] The central bank's focus remains on currency volatility and potential financial instability related to Seoul housing
